hypothesis: If tensor completion methods (CPD-S, NeAT, and their ensemble) are used as surrogate models for lattice structure design, then they will yield higher predictive accuracy (R²) and greater robustness to sparse or biased (non-uniform) training sampling for Young's modulus and mass-normalized Young's modulus than baseline ML methods (Gaussian Process, XGBoost, MLP) because tensor completion leverages the multi-way structure of the design parameter tensor to better generalize from limited and biased observations.
